task_completion.description: The HTML file is saved as math_quiz.html in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor showing the file path '/home/ga/Documents/math_quiz.html', a file manager showing the file 'math_quiz.html' in the Documents folder, or a terminal command confirming its creation at that exact path.

task_completion.description: The quiz includes the title and heading '4th Grade Math Challenge'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text '4th Grade Math Challenge' rendered as a large heading in a web browser, or within <title> and <h1> (or similar heading) tags in the HTML code.

task_completion.description: The quiz contains the five specified math questions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact questions: '7 x 8', '144 / 12', '15 x 13', '256 / 16', and '3 x (4 + 5)' rendered on the page or written in the HTML code.

task_completion.description: The quiz has text input fields for answers and a 'Check Answers' button.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for five text input boxes (or <input type='text'> tags) and a button labeled 'Check Answers' (or <button>Check Answers</button>) in the screenshots.

task_completion.description: The quiz includes JavaScript logic to grade the inputs and display a score.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for JavaScript code (<script>) checking the inputs against the correct answers (56, 12, 195, 16, 27) and calculating a score, or a rendered message on the page showing a score like 'You got X out of 5 correct!' after the button is clicked.

task_completion.description: The quiz includes basic CSS styling.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for <style> tags with CSS rules in the code, or a rendered page that is visually styled (e.g., non-default fonts, background colors, padding, or centered layout).

integrity.description: The agent created a genuine HTML file with code.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for HTML tags (<html>, <body>, etc.) in a text editor, or the file being successfully rendered as a web page in a browser.

integrity.description: The agent implemented actual JavaScript logic rather than hardcoding a static success message.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a <script> block containing variables, functions, and conditional statements for grading, rather than just a static HTML paragraph with the score.

integrity.description: The agent did not just display the correct answers as text on the page without requiring user input.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the answers (56, 12, 195, 16, 27) are used in the grading logic (JavaScript) and not just printed out as the visible text of the quiz questions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file path is '/home/ga/Documents/math_quiz.html'. The expected title is '4th Grade Math Challenge'. The correct answers to the math questions are 56, 12, 195, 16, and 27. The questions must include the numbers 144 and 256.
